title: Imperial Space
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Axe}}
[[File:Reconquest_of_the_Rim.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The borders of Imperial Space at the time of the [[Reconquest of the Rim]]]]
{{Quote|Attention all Ripoblus and Dimok craft, this is Imperial space. You are hereby ordered to stand down or face the consequences!|An [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] to [[Dimok (people)|Dimok]] and [[Ripoblus (people)|Ripoblus]] forces in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]|<ref name="TF">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>}}
'''Imperial Space''', later called '''Imperial Sector''' and '''Bastion space''', was the term given to the region of space controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and later the [[Imperial Remnant]] and the [[Fel Empire]]. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], it consisted of almost all the known [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. This term was mainly used after the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] became the dominant galactic government.

==History==
===Little Empires: 7–8 ABY===
[[File:Empire_7-8_ABY.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Imperial Space and some [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]]' territories]]
After the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|fall of Coruscant]] and the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] lost the status of galactic government. At this time, most remaining Imperial territory was shared between [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]].

The true Imperial territory included some sectors compressed between [[Zsinj's Empire]] and the [[Pentastar Alignment]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], a region at the south of the [[Pentastar Alignment]] from the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] to the Outer Rim and some [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] separated from those areas, throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

After the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|death of Warlord Zsinj]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Teren Rogriss]] managed to recapture a small part of [[Zsinj's Empire]] along the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] but he was quickly [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|pushed back]].

===Thrawn's Empire : 9 ABY===
[[File:ThrawnsCampaigns-TEA.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Thrawn's confederation|Thrawn's Empire]]]]
During his short but prolific [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] conquered a very large amount of [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] and [[Star system/Legends|star systems]], expanding [[Thrawn's confederation|his empire]] in all regions of the galaxy, from the Outer Rim Territories to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. At the moment of [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|his death]], he controlled more than a third of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], an equivalent territory to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The Empire would never again be as large until the rise of the [[Fel Empire]].

===The Dark Empire: 10–11 ABY===
[[File:Dark_empire_map.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The [[Dark Empire]] (purple), including the [[Pentastar Alignment]] (red)]]
After having come back from death, the reborn [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] unified the various warlords and Imperial factions and launched [[Operation Shadow Hand]] in order to reimpose his rule on the galaxy. Using his [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]], he reconquered an amount of territory spanning from the Outer Rim to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. At this time, the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]] was a region that separated the New Republic from the Empire.

===The Crimson Empire: 11 ABY===
This term referred to the color of the [[Armor/Legends|armor]] of the main Imperial leader of this period: [[Carnor Jax]]. Quickly attacked by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] after his takeover, the former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] and his [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends#The .22Council of Blood.22|successors]] lost much of the reborn Palpatine's territory. They were only able to hold onto a strip of of territory that stretched from the Outer Rim to the Colonies. In addition, the [[Pentastar Alignment]] and other [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] regained their independence after Palpatine's death. After the fall of the Crimson Empire, the New Republic proclaimed that the true Empire had ceased to exist, as all that was left were the feuding warlords in the Deep Core, various Moffs that were more interested in entrenching themselves on heavily defended fortress worlds, and the Pentastar Alignment, which had no intention of engaging the New Republic.

===Pellaeon's Empire: 12–17 ABY===
[[File:Pellaeon_Empire_map.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Imperial Remnant|Pellaeon's Empire]]]]
Pellaeon's Empire was the name given to Imperial Space in 12 ABY, after the [[Imperial Reunification]] and the formation of the Imperial Remnant. After the Conference at [[Tsoss Beacon/Legends|Tsoss Beacon]], Admiral Pellaeon moved the united Deep Core forces to the Outer Rim Territories, stretching as far as [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], and limited at the east by the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]]. This newly established fiefdom included territory from the former Crimson Empire, the former Ciutric Hegemony, what had formerly comprised the Pentastar Alignment, Moff fortress worlds, as well as semi-independent sectors such as Admiral Gaen Drommel's Oplovis Sector and Moff Getelles' Antemeridian Sector.

===The eight "backwater" sectors: 17–29 ABY===
{{Quote|We still hold eight sectors-over a thousand inhabited systems. We have the Fleet, nearly two hundred Star Destroyers strong. We're still very much a force to be reckoned with.|[[Ardiff]]|<ref name="Specter of the Past">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>}}
[[File:PentastarAlignment-TEA.jpg|thumb|250px|The eight "backwater" [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] and the former [[Pentastar Alignment]]]]
In response to the [[Imperial Skirmishes|Imperial aggression]] in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] launched an important campaign employing the full might of the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Fleet]]. The Imperial forces were decimated and Imperial Space was subsequently reduced to eight sectors. This situation forced Pellaeon to negotiate for peace with the New Republic in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].
*[[Braxant sector/Legends|Braxant sector]]
*[[Carrion sector/Legends|Carrion sector]]
*Half of [[Clacis sector/Legends|Clacis sector]]
*[[Dynali sector/Legends|Dynali sector]]
*[[Obtrexta sector/Legends|Obtrexta sector]]
*[[Perinn sector/Legends|Perinn sector]]
*Half of [[Prefsbelt sector/Legends|Prefsbelt sector]]
*[[Velcar sector/Legends|Velcar sector]]

===The "Galactic Empire": From 30 ABY===
At the end of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Imperial Remnant reconquered what territory it had lost to the Yuuzhan Vong, as well as an important number of sectors aligned to the New Republic before the war. Thanks to the weakness of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], the Imperial Remnant kept the sectors. At this time, the Imperial Remnant was sometimes referred to as the Galactic Empire.
*The former eight "backwater" sectors:
